title: About the Office of Research Administration

key_facts.msu_annual_research_funding: $50 million+ in annual grants and contracts
key_facts.ora_former_name: Office of Sponsored Programs and Research (OSPR)

content:
The Office of Research Administration (ORA), formerly the Office of Sponsored Programs and Research (OSPR), is Morgan State University's central coordinating unit for externally sponsored research. ORA processes, monitors, and facilitates the evaluation of all sponsored research projects to ensure compliance with institutional goals and procedures.

Morgan State University, founded in 1867 as the Centenary Biblical Institute, has grown over 150+ years into a Carnegie-classified R2 doctoral research institution with over 11,000 students, 25 doctoral programs, 27 master's programs, and over $50 million in annual grants and contracts. Reaching Carnegie's R1 classification is included in the University's 2021-2030 Strategic Plan, with efficient ORA services identified as integral to achieving this goal.

ORA assists MSU researchers — faculty and students alike — by:
  (1) providing access to critical information needed for grant submission,
  (2) offering grant-writing training materials,
  (3) sharing resources about post-award grant management,
  (4) disseminating information on research funding and productivity metrics including grant submissions, commitments, authorizations, and publications,
  (5) gathering researcher feedback for continuous improvement.

The ORA website is organized into the following sections: About, Mission & Vision, History, Staff Directory, Pre-Award, Post-Award, Policies and Guidelines, Research Compliance, Trainings, Resources, Funding Sources, and ORA Announcements.
